content:
# Digital Rock Physics

**Summary**: A technique using 3D images of rock to simulate and predict petrophysical properties.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2019-g201703430kfupmedusa|g201703430@kfupm.edu.sa, 2019]] applied digital-rock-physics to study flow characteristics in sandstone and carbonate rocks.

- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] applied digital-rock-physics to organic-rich samples to understand micro-scale factors like kerogen connectivity.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] applied digital-rock-physics to organic-rich samples to understand micro-scale factors like kerogen connectivity.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- DRP techniques could be a reliable complement for routine laboratory experiments. ([[2019-g201703430kfupmedusa|g201703430@kfupm.edu.sa, 2019, p. 19]])

- Digital rock physics revealed that specific surface area and kereogen connectivity are key micro-scale factors affecting elastic properties in unconventional reservoirs. ([[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024, p. 27]])
